skill.name: suggesting-tooling
skill.description: Analyzes a codebase and recommends project-specific Claude Code skills and subagents for recurring development workflows.
skill.summary: Suggests useful Claude Code skills and agents based on codebase patterns, gaps, and workflow signals.
skill.author: BrendanShields

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell, filesystem, sensitive-file, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found these are Markdown examples, YAML templates, and local repository inspection commands. No prompt injection, malware intent, network exfiltration, credential harvesting, persistence, or real cryptographic implementation was found. The remaining risk is low because the skill asks the assistant to inspect project files and may create Claude tooling only after user approval.

low_findings.title: Local Repository Inspection Commands
low_findings.description: The external command findings are command examples for listing project files, detecting package files, and checking existing Claude tooling. They do not include dynamic command construction, user-controlled shell input, network transfer, or persistence. Users should still review command execution in sensitive repositories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are static Markdown command examples limited to find, ls, and grep operations. The semantic context is codebase discovery for tooling recommendations, not secret access or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Sensitive File Reference Is Descriptive
low_findings.description: The .env reference is a project signal for deciding whether a security-review agent might be useful. It is not an instruction to open, copy, print, or transmit secret values.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The location appears inside a signals list for matching project patterns. No evidence found of commands or instructions that read .env contents.

low_findings.title: Device File Pattern Is Error Redirection
low_findings.description: The filesystem findings point to shell redirection that suppresses ls errors. This is benign local command behavior and not direct device-file access for data theft or persistence.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only device-like pattern is 2>/dev/null after ls commands. This redirects stderr and does not read or write sensitive project data.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto static hits do not correspond to cryptographic code. The cited locations are front matter, text about descriptions, or YAML table context in documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, encryption, random generation, or cryptographic API at these locations. The scanner matched documentation text rather than executable code.

content.value_statement: Teams often miss opportunities to automate repeated coding workflows with focused skills and agents. This skill inspects project signals and recommends practical Claude Code tooling with clear rationale.
actual_capabilities: Detects languages, frameworks, test tools, CI files, Docker usage, and existing Claude configuration.
Maps project signals to suggested skills such as testing, deployment, documentation, linting, and migrations.
Maps complex workflows to suggested agents such as code review, security review, API testing, and infrastructure analysis.
Ranks suggestions by priority and explains why each item would help the project.
Checks for existing skills and agents before recommending new tooling.
Provides context for creating approved skills or agents through separate creation workflows.
limitations: Recommendations depend on visible repository files and may miss workflows outside the codebase.
It does not validate generated skills or agents for correctness after creation.
It requires user approval before generating new tooling.
It is designed for Claude Code conventions and may need adaptation for other environments.

use_cases.title: Bootstrap AI Tooling for a New Repository
use_cases.description: Review a new codebase and identify the first skills or agents that would save repeated manual work.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Development Workflows
use_cases.description: Find gaps in testing, review, documentation, and deployment support across a shared project.

use_cases.title: Audit Existing Claude Code Setup
use_cases.description: Compare current .claude skills and agents against repository patterns to avoid duplicate or missing tooling.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Tooling Scan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a quick recommendation list.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this repository and suggest Claude Code skills or agents that would improve the development workflow. Explain each recommendation briefly.

prompt_templates.title: Gap-Focused Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request that avoids duplicate tooling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the existing .claude configuration, compare it with this repository, and suggest only missing skills or agents with clear evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Priority Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prioritized tooling roadmap for this project. Separate P1, P2, and P3 suggestions and explain expected workflow impact.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Approved Tooling Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: From the approved suggestions, prepare generation context for each skill or agent. Include project signals, intended purpose, and expected triggers.

output_examples.input: A TypeScript API with Jest tests, Express routes, Dockerfile, and no .claude folder.
output: P1 testing-code skill because tests exist and repeated test analysis is likely useful.
P1 api-testing agent because the project has REST routes that need contract validation.
P2 deploying-code skill because Docker exists but deployment automation may be project-specific.

output_examples.input: A Python service with migrations, GitHub Actions, and an existing documentation skill.
output: P2 db-migrations skill because migration files indicate a recurring database workflow.
No documentation suggestion because existing tooling already covers that area.
P3 linting-code skill only if lint configuration exists and no pre-commit workflow covers it.

output_examples.input: A regulated finance application with OAuth middleware and manual pull request reviews.
output: P1 security-review agent because authentication and regulated-domain signals require judgment.
P1 code-reviewer agent because manual reviews can benefit from consistent checks.
Domain-specific agent suggestion only if repository terminology confirms a focused need.

best_practices: Run the scan from the repository root so framework, test, and CI signals are visible.
Approve only suggestions that map to repeated workflows with clear evidence.
Review generated skills and agents before relying on them in normal development work.
anti_patterns: Creating generic skills that duplicate built-in assistant behavior.
Generating agents for simple one-step tasks that a skill can handle.
Accepting suggestions without checking existing .claude tooling first.

faq.question: Does this skill create tooling automatically?
faq.answer: No. It presents recommendations first and requires user approval before any creation workflow is used.

faq.question: What files does it inspect?
faq.answer: It looks for repository signals such as package files, test configuration, CI files, Docker files, and existing .claude folders.

faq.question: Can it help with both skills and agents?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests skills for predictable tasks and agents for workflows that need analysis or judgment.

faq.question: Will it replace existing project tools?
faq.answer: No. It is designed to identify gaps and avoid duplicate suggestions when existing tooling is already present.

faq.question: Is it specific to Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. Its templates and recommendations are centered on Claude Code skills and subagents.

faq.question: How should recommendations be reviewed?
faq.answer: Check the evidence, priority, and expected workflow value before approving any generated skill or agent.
